nodes.context: Chechnya-based paramilitary forces loyal to Ramzan Kadyrov, involved in human rights abuses and international conflicts.
Former separatist fighters from the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria, many of whom were pardoned and joined the Kadyrovites.
Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014.
Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014.
Ukrainian National Guard unit based in Mariupol, known for far-right ideology and fighting against Russian forces.
Pro-Russian separatist militia in the Luhansk region of Ukraine.
A Kadyrovite unit headed by Adam Delimkhanov, involved in kidnappings.
Former FSB spetsnaz unit headed by Movladi Baisarov, part of Kadyrovites until conflict with Ramzan Kadyrov.
Spetsnaz GRU unit headed by Said-Magomed Kakiev, sided with Baisarov against Kadyrovites.

edges.context: Many Kadyrovites were former Ichkeria militants pardoned and integrated into Kadyrov's forces.
Vostok Battalion is a Chechen unit loyal to Kadyrov, part of Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.
Chechen Death Battalion is a Chechen unit loyal to Kadyrov, part of Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.
Azov Regiment fighters insulted Chechen troops by greasing bullets in lard, referencing Islamic prohibition of pork.
Chechen troops reportedly executed conscripted LPR soldiers who refused to fight.
Goretz was a detachment of the Kadyrovites until a conflict with Ramzan Kadyrov.
The Oil Regiment is a Kadyrovite unit headed by Adam Delimkhanov.
Special Battalion Zapad sided with Baisarov against Kadyrovites in a 2006 confrontation.
